programme.desc: Investigators initially accepted a confession from the father of a six-year-old girl who disappeared but do not discover the truth until 10 years later(n)

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: How investigators managed to lift a handprint from the body of a murder victim using superglue fumes, and so gained enough evidence to catch her killer(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: After three murders with chillingly similar scenarios occurred in Austin, Texas, police began to wonder if they were chasing a serial killer(n)

programme.title: Cold Blood
programme.desc: Police are suspicious of Kristine Fitzhugh's 'accidental' death falling down the stairs. After proving that she was brutally murdered, her husband becomes the prime suspect(n)

programme.title: Murder By the Sea
programme.desc: The murder of a part-time actress triggers a nationwide manhunt for a killer(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Cameron's work at the German PoW camp brings him into conflict with one of the prisoners, while Janet fears Dr Finlay's affection for a married patient could jeopardise his career. Medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gabriel learns Smithy reported him over Kerry's rape allegation and the two come to blows - but retribution comes from an unexpected source when the corrupt copper is gunned down by the Sun Hill sniper. Elsewhere, suspicion falls on Shirley after Yvonne's niece accidentally swallows an Ecstasy tablet, Ramani fears Terry is letting his personal preoccupations interfere with his police work, and a shamefaced Jim tells his colleagues that June has left him(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit PC Leela Kapoor proves her mettle on her first day at Sun Hill, but the exposure of a dark secret from her past sours her working relationship with Sheelagh. Jim contemplates stepping outside the law to pay off his gambling debts, while another shooting by the serial sniper leads to an influx of armed officers - one of whom, Sergeant Mark Rollins, just happens to be Lance's boyfriend(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jim sinks to a new low when he pawns valuable police evidence to raise the cash he needs to pay off his gambling debts - then promptly blows the lot on a horse. Andrea refuses to abandon her undercover assignment even though Adam is getting uncomfortably close to the truth. Smithy finds the sniper's rifle and it appears to be only a matter of time before the culprit is finally caught - but not everything is as it seems(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road traffic accident turns into a drugs investigation when a quantity of cocaine is found in the crashed vehicle. The driver is dead, but Yvonne is more interested in his passenger, none other than Shirley Moss, who's suffered two broken legs. However, when she begins to question the errant teenager about her continued involvement in drugs, Yvonne soon has reason to believe the youngster isn't the architect of her own downfall - especially after the PC's sister, Beverley, is spotted entering a dealer's flat being staked out by Ken and Suzie. Elsewhere, Mark is upset that his colleagues in SO19 have discovered he is gay, but with the secret now out he throws caution to the wind and decides to take his relationship with Lance one step further - it looks like Sun Hill is about to witness its first gay wedding. With romance in the air, the Christmas party gets into full swing, and new girl Amber certainly enjoys herself - and causes much gossip - by very publicly kissing one of the barm(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuthing housewife investigates a spiritualist who appears to be blackmailing her customers, while Geoffrey falls in love, but his secret romance threatens to undermine Hetty's carefully laid trap - and nearly causes him to quit the agency(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A middle-aged widow's family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether he's a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives of Nonnatus House return, with new nurse Barbara, who is anxious to prove herself - only to get off on the wrong foot after a late-night drinks party. A family of severely neglected children is discovered, so Trixie strives to restore their health and bring happiness into their lives, leaving Tom filled with admiration for the woman he loves, while Sister Evangelina finally undergoes tests for her abdominal pains. Shelagh finds she is at the surgery almost as much as she is at home, so the Turners come up with a solution which, for 1960, proves somewhat ground-breaking(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Thomas and Clarissa are just settling down for their respective Christmas Day celebrations when they are called in to investigate a shooting spree at an enormous property owned by Andy McMorris, the CEO of a haulage company. Emilia Fox stars(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Boyd learns he is being removed from the Cold Case unit. For his final job, he re-opens an investigation into the disappearance of 16 homeless teenagers between 1979 and 1982 - the first case he ever worked on. However, as the team struggles to find any new information, Grace worries about her colleague's reasons for revisiting his past - while a guilt-ridden Sarah becomes desperate to make amends for her role in Boyd's sacking. First part of the crime drama's two-part final story, starring Trevor Eve, with Paul McGann(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Peasant girl Tess Durbeyfield is encouraged by her father to seek advancement with their supposed relatives, the wealthy D'Urbervilles, and meets the charming Alec, man of the house since his father's death. He showers her with attention and gifts, promising her family will want for nothing - but as she soon discovers to her cost, there's a dark side to her persistent new suitor. Thomas Hardy adaptation, starring Gemma Arterton, Hans Matheson, Ian Puleston-Davies and Anna Massey(n)

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Cameron's work at the German PoW camp brings him into conflict with one of the prisoners, while Janet fears Dr Finlay's affection for a married patient could jeopardise his career. Medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Wealthy widow Alice Costigan invites Robert to stay at her country house for a spot of pheasant shooting, filling him with high hopes of romance. However, he's not the only one vying for her affections, and when her nephew falls ill she snubs him by turning to his rival for help. Victorian medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder, Maureen Beattie and Andrew Connolly(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: As the regiment prepares to leave for the South Atlantic to defend Deliverance Island, Andy contemplates proposing, and Sarah and Jeremy's relationship begins to crumble(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: It's a difficult day for Yvonne as she faces up to the fact that she was wrong to throw Shirley out of the house. However, the youngster eventually accepts her apology and it looks as if, once she has pulled through this latest mishap, the kindly PC will be able to get her life back on track - until fate deals a terrible blow. Meanwhile, with the tensions in his relationship with Andrea beginning to take their toll, Neil is devastated at the news that she has been critically injured. He turns up at the hospital, only to be faced by Smithy, having already filled his shoes. Lance finally accepts Mark's proposal of marriage after a long day of deliberation, while Gina is surprised to catch a glimpse of Irene Radford. She is convinced her eyes are deceiving her - until Jack confides in her that Sun Hill's crime matriarch is indeed back. Michele Austin, Andrew Lancel, Simon Rouse, Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham and Stefan Booth star(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark decides to leave when he realises neither Tricia nor Chrissie want him around - but an encounter with a patient could change their minds. Donna applies for a bar job, and persuades Mickie to lie to the manager about her experience. Mubbs gets a wake-up call when Owen decides not to let him be best man, and Sean offers to stand by Jess(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Mark and George treat a youngster who has suffered an epileptic fit, while in something of a quandary having spent the night together. A family dispute culminates in a reckless car chase and horrific crash, while Baz misses Charlie's presence(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Having discovered Irene Radford is back, Gina is determined to put her old enemy behind bars once and for all - and the two are soon face to face again when an intruder at the Radford house turns out to be Irene herself, who claims she has returned because she is critically ill with cancer. However, the police have got wind that she is plotting one final raid before retiring from a life of crime for good, so Phil is tasked to monitor a prison meeting between Irene and her son David, where the top brass are hoping he'll glean valuable information about the job. Unfortunately, the philandering DS is easily distracted by an old flame working at the prison and misses half the conversation - so when the raid gets under way, it soon becomes clear they've been duped, and a senior officer ends up staring down the barrel of Irene's gun. Back at the station, Honey is upset to learn that Amber has taken the 'shy and sensitive' Steve Hunter's virginity, while Sam seems to be warming to Phil. Is ro(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat is determined not to let Frank stand in the way of her independence - but Ricky pays the price. Michelle is on tenterhooks as she awaits the results of her exams(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark is in for a shock(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A drug war leads to a fire in a block of flats in a nearby estate. Meanwhile, Sicknote gives a public speech which leads to him receiving a standing ovation(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A friend who runs a fairground asks Lovejoy to sell silver candlesticks on his behalf, but the police arrive and reveal they are stolen property. Jimmy Jewel and Phil Daniels guest star, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Animal rights activists free a group of monkeys, one of which is carrying a lethal and contagious virus, so Jim tries to contain the infection before Jersey is struck by an epidemic. John Nettles stars(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Grandad receives a valuable present from Jack and Adrian for his 75th birthday, while Joey is reunited with his long-lost dog(n)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Spooner reveals unexpected musical abilities - and the rest of the staff, astonished to discover he has any talent at all, insist on cashing in on his chart success. Mike Berry, John Inman, Frank Thornton, Wendy Richard and Mollie Sugden star in the final episode of the long-running shopfloor comedy, which features a guest appearance by former Crimewatch presenter Nick Ross(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: A Hollywood producer arrives in town, full of big ideas about filming one of Lionel's books - but his plan to use authentic locations is met with disapproval. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora Batty spots Alvin with a mystery woman, and convinces herself that the couple are becoming close after some devious work by the elderly Lothario and a blushing Billy. Pearl spots a lonely hearts advertisement that she is sure is from Howard. Kathy Staff, Brian Murphy and Keith Clifford star(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The duo investigate the murder of a solicitor in a sleepy Yorkshire village, but grumpy detective Dalziel finds himself personally involved when a letter addressed to him is discovered among the victim's belongings. Drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan as the chalk-and-cheese detectives(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A dying man's last desperate answerphone message confuses the police and hampers their investigation into his murder. Adding to the air of mystery surrounding the case, it also sets his son off on a nightmare journey. Glasgow-set detective drama, starring Mark McManus, James Macpherson, Blythe Duff, Iain Anders, Harriet Buchan and Robert Robertson(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Wealthy widow Alice Costigan invites Robert to stay at her country house for a spot of pheasant shooting, filling him with high hopes of romance. programme.desc: Marsham learns his wife has breast cancer and tries to make amends for neglecting her, shelving a planned move to Scotland and begging Robert and Eleanor to perform a life-saving operation. Medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave and David Calder(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: A remote British-ruled island in the South Pacific is invaded and the King's Own Fusiliers find themselves part of the task force sent to retake it. Unfortunately, Corporal Hobbs is soon captured and tortured by enemy troops, and Colonel Eastwood's daring plan of attack has explosive consequences(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Having discovered Irene Radford is back, Gina is determined to put her old enemy behind bars once and for all - and the two are soon face to face again when an intruder at the Radford house turns out to be Irene herself, who claims she has returned because she is critically ill with cancer. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On a busy night behind the bar, Mickie notices Jodie is becoming breathless and feverish, and tries to persuade her to seek medical help. Tricia and Mark find working together tense, but agree to be as professional as possible, while Diane suffers from pre-wedding jitters and turns to Ric for support. Her fears may be well-founded, as Owen's stag night ends with him kissing Chrissie(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A young rollerblade hockey player is coy about how he received his mysterious injuries, Liz's boyfriend leaves her for another woman, and Tina acts beyond the call of duty when she contacts Social Services about an injured boy(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following the Radford family's botched attempt to spring David from prison, Irene drives off with Gina at gunpoint and forces the inspector to let her colleagues know she means business. If they don't give up the chase, she won't hesitate to pull the trigger. Soon, Gina finds herself alone with her enemy, and as they begin to talk, the two of them realise they understand one another better than they could possibly have imagined. However, when Irene orders the shooting of Jack Gaunt, it becomes clear Gina is still in very real danger. Meanwhile, Honey has it out with Steve, disappointed that he had sex with Amber, and accusing him of being as bad as his brother. Indeed, Phil is having his fair share of problems as Sam discovers that his New Year's invitation followed hard on the heels of his dalliance with the prison liaison officer. Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham, Lisa Maxwell and Scott Maslen star(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pete takes time off to treat Christine to a day at the races, Mandy is the subject of gossip in the Square, and Dot interferes in Mark's life and gives Hattie shocking new(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: PatCabs needs another driver, Rachel and Kathy grab some bargains at the jumble sale, and Michelle gets a job offer. Sharon is impressed with Grant's hard work at the Vic(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Blue Watch are called to a DHSS office where a distressed claimant has covered himself in petrol and set himself alight. Starring Glen Murphy(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: While searching a Cambridge antiques fair for religious books with Charlotte and Charlie Gimbert, Lovejoy discovers a rare Bible that seems to offer a solution to all his problems. Meanwhile, Tinker has a hard time persuading Beth's father that she should carry on working for such a rogue. Drama, guest starring Richard Briers and James Ellis, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim goes against his better judgement to help Philippa Vale - aka the Ice Maiden - discover who is trying to kill her. Drama, guest starring Liza Goddard, with John Nettles and Terence Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Freddie loses a valuable ring, while Jack, who found it and tried to pawn it, is filled with remorse. Meanwhile, Adrian shocks the family with his radical change in personality, insisting on being called Jimmy. Comedy, starring Jonathon Morris, Ronald Forfar, Victor McGuire and Jean Boht(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: One of the yellowcoat's professionalism is questioned by a sharp-eyed passer-by who spots her entering a staff chalet late at night. With Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Gillian Taylforth(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley presents a programme following the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, beginning with the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a fire-damaged house in West Sussex, a semi-detached in the West Midlands and a barn conversion in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, making sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and cracking a gold bangle scam in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the origins of an underground seaside grotto(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha hosts her first chronic disease coffee morning, while Daniel tries to get through to Jimmi in prison. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Return of the comedy drama about a pair of Stratford-upon-Avon private detectives, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple returning from 20 years living in America as they look for a home in Hampshire's New Forest near to their children's new school. Sonali also goes behind the scenes during the preparations for a hugely popular traditional country show(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeeper Valentine and market gardener John meet their regular customers and discover new ones selling their skincare products and organic vegetables at St George's weekly market in central Belfast(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram scour Kent for items to sell at auction in Rayleigh, Essex, along the way buying a French sunburst clock and an old school bell(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Anna Williamson reveals what is being done to help new mothers who have mental health issues. Plus, the programme discovers how London's club scene is going plastic free, and there is a report on vets helping dogs on the capital's streets(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda has a run-in with Shelley at the school gates and is furious to discover Mick has cancelled her card - but her day only gets worse from there, leading the drunken landlady to inadvertently put young Ollie in danger. Denise returns home from Scotland and makes amends with Sheree, Jack tells Kat the police have evidence that could help Kush and Iqra invites herself along to a Panesar family meal(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme meets some of the growing number of people living in temporary accommodation. With a shortage of council-owned properties, and a desire to keep people out of bed and breakfasts, local authorities are increasingly turning to the private sector for help. In 2013, a change in the law meant companies could buy up and convert old office blocks into homes without planning permission. Reporter Callum Tulley explores life for residents living in these converted spaces(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thomas investigates the case of John Sealy, a soldier who died during a military training exercise involving exposure to CS gas. It appears the victim died from an aneurysm, but when Thomas discovers the trace of a possible nerve agent in his system, the man's family are desperate for answers. Was a chemical weapon involved, or is there a more innocent explanation? First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Vlogger Shu Lin explores the history of the Chinese community in the UK from the 1950s through to the present, day, revealing how these migrants transformed Britain's bland post-war dining scene. She finds out how an area of Soho became today's vibrant Chinatown and traces how the community has transformed from relying heavily on the restaurant trade, to one achieving outstanding business and educational success(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, who is appearing as Doctor Robotnik in si-fi adventure Sonic the Hedgehog. Also on the sofa is Australian actress Margot Robbie, who features in new DC superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go, and another bunch of intrepid audience members tries to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: An audacious scam robs NHS hospitals of more than £12.5million and a hospital cashier leads the high life, taking money meant for patients and putting it into her own bank account(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A learner driver tries to cheat his way through his driving test using a Bluetooth device, while a wealthy businessman sells unlicensed medicines that he claims can cure cancer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Jonathan Pratt and Danny Sebastian travel to Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire for the buying and selling contest. Two married couples go head to head to buy three items that will hopefully make a profit at auction, and they all have their sights set on a golden gavel. Eric also visits the small village of Eyam to discover how, 350 years ago, the villagers made a daring decision to quarantine themselves, and stop the spread of the plague(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al tries to cure Bear of his fear of blood, as Daniel and Zara continue to work through their troubled past. On a drug raid, Rob bumps into two familiar faces(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu take on the case of a washed-up rock star who believes that the Devil is trying to kill him, and set out to unmask the real culprit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Worcestershire to find a home in Cornwall, and discovers a project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers Ian and Fiona compete against the Wills family, who run a mixed farm and exhibit pigs, at the Yeovil Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram look for bargains in East Anglia, as well as having a go at step dancing and discovering an 18th-century collection of self-playing instruments(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is racked with guilt about Kush and plans to put things right, despite Tiffany urging her not to do anything that puts her in danger. Shirley is determined to make Linda face her problems and pleads with Mick to take his wife to an addict support session at the community centre. Ash and her brothers are surprised by Suki's reaction to Iqra, Sheree opens up to Patrick about Isaac, and Sharon makes a peace offering to Dennis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Oxford United v Newcastle United (Kick-off 8.05pm). Coverage of the fourth-round replay, which comes from Kassam Stadium, as the teams contest the right to face West Bromwich Albion at The Hawthorns in the next round. The match at St James' Park ended 0-0, with Newcastle forward Joelinton having two goals disallowed for offside, and League One Oxford will now feel they have an excellent chance of producing an upset against their Premier League opponents(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke have one day to tame a wild, riverside patch belonging to a houseboat in Newhaven, Sussex(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler tries to become self-sufficient in fruit and vegetables as she uses her small back garden to plant, grow and harvest her own produce. She begins with peas and beans, and provides useful growing tips as well as recipes including broad-bean falafels and pea-shoot cocktails. She also forages for willow to make plant supports and buys two chickens(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Helen Flanagan and Jorgie Porter scour some of Scotland's finest antique shops in the hope of finding items that will make the biggest profit at auction in Stirlingshire. Jorgie takes a detour to hit an ace at on a very historic tennis court, while Helen takes a break from the shops to learn about tartan and kilts. Experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton try to keep the duo on the straight and narrow(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Reports on rural developments from the Peak District. Anita Rani finds out about a campaign to save Bamford's village pub and joins a group of ramblers on a trip into the surrounding countryside. Sean Fletcher meets a grocer who pulls together all the best produce from local farmers, while Helen Skelton goes rock climbing. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes some new arrivals on the farm and Tom Heap reports on the financial impact of environmentally friendly farming(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Mary visits Goodwood House, home to the March family, famous for their iconic horse racing and motoring events. She is taken on a thrilling lap of Goodwood motor circuit by Lord March, before attending one of his infamous dinner parties where 300 guests dine in style in splendid state rooms. Mary also learns more about the Estate's organic farm, before preparing a Coq-au-Vin and a four-tier cake for a cricket tea set in one of the oldest cricket clubs in the world(n)

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: The Hairy Biker fulfils a boyhood dream by visiting the pyramids at Giza, goes to the oasis of Al Fayoum in search of a dish with mythical properties and heads to a place on the banks of the Nile where ancient man first turned to farming. To mark the end of his journey, Dave is invited by a family of Nubians for a feast to celebrate the end of Eid Al Adha, and helps prepare a traditional meal that has become famous across the entire Arab world(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Novak Djokovic v Dominic Thiem. Sue Barker presents action from the men's singles final on day 14 of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year. Djokovic defeated Rafael Nadal in straight sets last year to win his seventh title here(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites veteran broadcaster and entertainer Nicholas Parsons to delve into his youth in Hampstead Heath. The chef cooks nostalgic dishes in an outdoor kitchen, hoping to transport Nicholas back to his mother's cooking and reflect the kind of food he likes eating now(n)

programme.title: The Train (1964)
programme.desc: As the Allies look set to liberate Paris from the Nazi forces, the Germans resolve to take all they can from the city - and plot to transport national treasures back to Berlin. However, bold French Resistance forces vow to stop them, enlisting the help of a train inspector along the way. Second World War adventure, starring Burt Lancaster, Paul Scofield, Jeanne Moreau and Michel Simon(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist explores some of the world's most dangerous environments, beginning with a journey to the Arctic. The objective is to discover how rising temperatures are affecting the environment, wildlife and the local people. Steve begins his expedition on the east coast of Greenland, where he assembles a team of experts to join him. The first stage of the journey involves kayaking across the world's largest fjord, with the explorers braving lethal and ever shifting sea ice, vast icebergs and hungry polar bears(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the World Cup meeting in Schladming, Austria, featuring the iconic night slalom. Plus, highlights of the world's longest boardercross downhill race, the Inferno from Feldberg, Germany(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The couples are split up for a navigation challenge that pits men against women on a trek through the wilderness. A local search and rescue expert oversees their efforts and offers his thoughts on which couple should be the next to be eliminated from the contest. After another pair has gone home, the remaining competitors are taken to a gun range to be introduced to the Alaskan attitude to firearms and put their marksmanship to the test(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Burnley v Arsenal at Turf Moor. Spurs and the Citizens shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw in August's reverse fixture, while the Gunners recorded a 2-1 home win over the Clarets. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: A preview of tonight's showpiece match between Kansas City Chiefs and San Francisco 49ers at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal at the Academy Stadium and Chelsea v West Ham United at Kingsmeadow, as City, Arsenal and Chelsea continued their three-way battle for the title(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of fixtures, featuring Ireland v Scotland at Energia Park, Wales v Italy at Cardiff Arms Park and France v England at Stade du Hameau(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Buxton, Derbyshire, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in St Albans - including a country lodge, a 1970s house, and an ultra-modern family home - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On the 75th Holocaust Memorial Day, Sacha's great aunt, a survivor of the concentration camps, is admitted to hospital. Her health is deteriorating severely, and Sacha is devastated by the realisation that she i unlikely to live through the day. Cameron is suspicious of a stabbing victim's boyfriend, only to discover he is hiding injuries of his own. Nicky has to step in when her mother receives threatening phone calls after failing to pay her debts(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The last semi-final sees the remaining hopefuls head to Ollie Dabbous's London restaurant Hide, where they receive a masterclass from the Michelin-starred chef in making his beef tartare. He then asks them to create a dish from the restaurant's select larder before they are each given responsibility for one of the dishes during a packed service. Back at the studio, a final test awaits in which the hopefuls must cook two dishes for Gregg Wallace, Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti, who decide who will go through to next week's finals(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of fixtures, featuring Ireland v Scotland at Energia Park, Wales v Italy at Cardiff Arms Park and France v England at Stade du Hameau(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Ann and Stephen want to leave the winter in Glasgow behind and rent a property in Benalmadena, southern Spain, with local agent Mike on hand to help them. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of the leading ladies of Hollywood's golden age, including Lauren Bacall, Natalie Wood, Debbie Reynolds, Joan Crawford and Bette Davis. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: The Red Shoes (1948)
programme.desc: A budding prima ballerina is set to achieve her dream of stardom when she decides to leave her prestigious company to marry a composer - a decision that enrages her possessive mentor, who vows revenge. Powell and Pressburger's drama, based on the story by Hans Christian Andersen, starring Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann and Albert Basserman(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Hull, Caroline Hawley is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews, and Nick Hall is dazzled by some jewellery. Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Thailand, finding out about the lifesaving work of Bangkok's Queen Saovabha Memorial Institute and ending in the resort of Hua Hin(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken put health theories to the test with the help of pairs of identical twins, revealing ways we can live a healthier life. This time, three pairs of identical twins take part in a series of experiments to find out if it is possible to improve your cognitive function. Meanwhile, other participants test out alternative methods to repel mosquitoes, and find out if you can get fit just by watching the television(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the Romanov dynasty, the life and works of Frederick Douglass, Harpo Marx, and Otis Redding. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Eggchasers take on the Turophiles and with both teams having won one game and lost another, this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising 1 kilohertz sine wave, Grawlixes, Mincing, Pixelation(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the quarter-finals, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches to progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part one of two. The classicist examines the nude in western art and its troubling power to provoke ideas about gender, sex and moral transgression. Mary starts with a statue of Aphrodite by Praxiteles and Titian's Venus of Urbino, asking how a woman like her should respond to the artworks, and considers the challenge of depicting the female nude for a woman artist by looking at the work of 17th-century painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In a life-drawing class, Mary joins a hen party as they attempt to sketch a naked man(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: The four match officials get ready to oversee a crunch clash between United and Rovers in the last game of the season, revealing a tale of promotion, relegation, corruption, and so, so much more. Return of the darkly comic anthology, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with David Morrissey and Ralf Little(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis and Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to review Super Bowl LIV, which saw Kansas City Chiefs face San Francisco 49ers at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families promote their speciality cheeses and different forms of garlic at the Scottish Game Fair, having worked hard to get their products to market(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Reports on rural developments from the Peak District. Anita Rani finds out about a campaign to save Bamford's village pub and joins a group of ramblers on a trip into the surrounding countryside. Sean Fletcher meets a grocer who pulls together all the best produce from local farmers, while Helen Skelton goes rock climbing. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes some new arrivals on the farm and Tom Heap reports on the financial impact of environmentally friendly farming(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram scour Kent for items to sell at auction in Rayleigh, Essex, along the way buying a French sunburst clock and an old school bell(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first episode of finals week, in which the four remaining contestants must cook for an exclusive gathering of some of the nation's greatest chefs. Each of the contenders takes responsibility for one course of the four-course meal, under the watchful eye of judges Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from the opening round of fixtures, including Toronto Wolfpack v Castleford Tigers, St Helens v Salford Red Devils and Leeds Rhinos v Hull FC(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: A Scottish duo look for a rental home on Madeira, with the help of local property expert Cecelia. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores the Welsh coast. Nick Crane investigates evidence that a devastating tsunami hit 400 years ago, and finds out why scientists planning a trip to Mars find the local landscape a surprisingly good stand-in for the red planet's surface. Alice Roberts tests the claim that the world's first powered flight was made by a Welsh carpenter seven years before the Wright brothers and Dick Strawbridge reveals how, in 1947, a man on holiday to Anglesey came up with the design for a new car that would conquer the world - the Land Rover(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television and film appearances made over the years by actress Deborah Kerr, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Black Narcissus (1947)
programme.desc: British nuns establish a school and hospital on a remote mountainside in the Himalayas, where the nursing sisters face isolation as well as native mistrust. However, it is the presence of a male land agent that takes a final toll on their peace of mind. Oscar-winning drama from directorial team Powell and Pressburger. With Deborah Kerr, David Farrar, Kathleen Byron, Flora Robson and Jean Simmons(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Christina Trevanion are in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, to assess a range of items, while presenter Paul Martin pays a visit to Nostell Priory near Wakefield(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Ho Chi Minh City to Halong Bay, and uncovers the traumatic 20th-century history of Vietnam, a country that went from a French colony to a socialist republic. Along the way, he braves the city streets on a motorcycle, samples French-Vietnamese fusion food, and tries his hand at the national sport(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A look at the smaller boats that fish off Cornwall's coast, often with only a single crew member. Despite being a sustainable method of fishing, this traditional approach is coming under threat from industrialisation, a lack of new talent taking up the trade, and declining stocks of mackerel and other once common fish. In Penberth, James is the only fisherman under 65 - and since traditional methods rely on boats supporting each other, he may have to give up when the older generation retires. On the Helford River, a veteran fisherman shares his concerns for the fish processing industry if Britain leaving the EU leads to a decline in foreign labour(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Universal Credit from the point of view of both government employees and those who rely on the controversial benefits system to support themselves. The first episode focuses on Peckham Jobcentre in London. Rachel, who left her job to care for her elderly parents, now struggles to support her family on Universal Credit while having to cope with debt and depression. Jobcentre employee Karen finds herself faced with similar difficulties to her clients, and has to take a second job to support herself. The programme also follows the difficulties faced by civil servants in overseeing sweeping changes to the benefits system(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is feeling the strain of her menopause. After a night out with the girls, she decides a trip to her doctor is in order(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedy performers Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson delve into the archive to parody a 1970s film on what to do at a business meeting(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis and Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Body Clock: What Makes Us Tick?
programme.desc: Everyone has an internal biological clock that governs their daily rhythms, including diet, general health and exercise patterns. But is it possible to manage the body clock better? To find out, former commando Aldo Kane is locked in an abandoned nuclear bunker for 10 days with no way of telling the time. He is monitored around the clock by evolutionary biologist Ella Al-Shamahi and a team of scientists as he carries out a barrage of tests to uncover exactly what makes the body clock tick. At the same time, two couples test the latest thinking on how to improve their lives, with general advice on sleeping better and managing their time(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal at the Academy Stadium and Chelsea v West Ham United at Kingsmeadow, as City, Arsenal and Chelsea continued their three-way battle for the title(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia through the seasons, beginning during spring in Nant Ffrancon in the Ogwen Valley. A mountain guide takes him high up above the glacial landscape of Cwm Idwal, before the wildlife expert heads to a hidden river gorge in full flow, listens to the dawn chorus in an ancient woodland, and visits Bird Rock, an imposing crag in the Dysynni Valley(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: From enormous tortoises and deep-diving lizards to fish-eating snakes and birds that hunt giant, venomous centipedes, the wildlife of the Galapagos Islands is unique and bizarre. However, the wilderness that inspired Charles Darwin and the theory of evolution is becoming ever-more crowded due to increased migration and tourism. David Attenborough narrates this documentary, which examines how the Pacific archipelago's animals are adapting to their changing circumstances(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Documentary using clips from the BBC archives to explore how six decades of TV has influenced the understanding of the arts. In the first edition, Kate Bryan looks at the use of the nude by both artists and film-makers as a means to shock their audiences and challenge conventions(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Investigation into attitudes toward nudity, suggesting people's embarrassment about their bodies can be traced back to a specific stage of evolution - the period when mankind's ancestors lost their fur. Scientists from Africa to America add an informed perspective, and volunteers take a series of tests designed to assess reactions to the naked human form, shedding new light on humanity's physical and social development(n)

programme.title: Fig Leaf: The Biggest Cover Up in History
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the history of the fig leaf's use throughout 2,000 years of Western art and ethics. He explains how the work of Michelangelo fuelled the Fig Leaf Campaign censorship effort, how Bernini created a new form of erotica with his use of the slipping gauze, and how Rodin brought depictions of nudity back to the public's attention. The presenter argues that Victorians had a more sophisticated and mature attitude to sexuality than is found today, and offers his view that modern art could be redeemed from cheap sensation by the return of the fig leaf(n)

programme.title: No Body's Perfect with Rankin and Alison Lapper
programme.desc: Disabled artist Alison Lapper and renowned photographer Rankin meet four people who do not conform to traditional notions of beauty, or who hate being photographed, and invite them to step in front of Rankin's camera, in this documentary tackling contemporary attitudes to image and identity(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Elton John, Willie Nelson, Merle Haggard, Alabama Shakes, Jack White, Nas, Ana Gabriel, Beck, Los Lobos and Steve Martin are among the modern artists who test their skills against the demands of the recording machine that literally made American music. There are no edits, no overdubs, and no retakes, and the disc only allows for three minutes of recording time. Despite these limitations, today's recordings for American Epic have one advantage - the freshly recorded sound is crystal clear and of an astonishing depth, transporting listeners vividly into the past - and the future. Narrated by Robert Redford(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric and Bobby conclude their journey around the United Kingdom by heading to Scotland, where they visit the Edinburgh birthplace of 19th-century scientist James Clerk Maxwell, whose pioneering work includes the 1865 theory of electromagnetism. The duo then analyse the science behind the deep-fried Mars Bar before heading to Glasgow explore the Hunterian Museum's collection of antiquarian experimental instruments and delve into the personal correspondence of mathematical physicist and engineer Lord Kelvin(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Italian architect Francesco da Mosto visits the historic Mostar Bridge, now rebuilt after being destroyed in the Yugoslav war. After sailing between the Croatian islands of Hvar and Korcula, he arrives in the walled city of Dubrovnik and visits the Franciscan monastery at its heart(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The history of love reaches the Victorian age, where Lucy explores the romantic gestures that emerged and still exist today, such as performing love songs and sending flowers. She also reveals how medieval chivalry shaped courtship, defining the roles that men and women had to play, and dips into popular novels of the time, discovering how the passion explored in fiction was translating into real-life desires(n)

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: Historian Dominic Sandbrook follows up his 2012 documentary series The 70s by moving on to the following decade. He begins by examining how powerful new forces of choice and consumerism radically reshaped British life - a new culture of consumer-driven populism that propelled Margaret Thatcher to victory. He takes in everything from the popularity of Delia Smith and affordable fashions on the high street, to the subcultures of Britain's youth and the crisis of identity that rocked and splintered the political left(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: First episode of the drama charting the lives of five flat-sharing lawyers in London. No-nonsense law graduate Anna goes for a job at a barristers' office - only to find an old college flame on the interview panel who offers her the position in return for a repeat performance of their last one-night stand. Starring Daniela Nardini, Andrew Lincoln, Jack Davenport, Amita Dhiri and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Miles is seduced by a woman facing fraud charges, ruining Anna's hopes of renewing their romance. Egg's dissatisfaction with his new responsibilities is deepened by Warren's apparent confidence, but the arrival of his cousin threatens to shatter the facade. Jason Hughes, Andrew Lincoln and Jack Davenport star(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Warren realises Delilah has bulimia and tries to help her, while Miles clashes with Anna, accusing her of jealousy. Egg lets himself get over-involved with a cancer patient who believes his condition was caused by the experimental drugs he was given as a child. Jack Davenport, Daniela Nardini and Andrew Lincoln star(n)

programme.title: Art of Germany
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of German art, visiting places of significance and looking at paintings, sculptures and other works. He begins his journey by travelling to the large cathedral in Cologne, which he believes encapsulates the varied character of the country's creative output. He also looks at the earliest paintings of the Northern Renaissance in Munich, a city that is home to the woodcuts of Albrecht Durer and the cosmic visions of Albrecht Altdorfer(n)

programme.title: Treasures of the Indus
programme.desc: Sona Datta examines the artistic legacy of the Mughal Empire, from the Taj Mahal to fabulously intricate miniatures of court life, and traces how it also sowed the seeds of discord(n)

programme.title: Tales of Tudor Travel: The Explorer's Handbook
programme.desc: Professor Nandini Das explores Hakluyt's Principal Navigations which records accounts of ventures in search of lucrative spices and dyes. It is a prototype for today's travel guides with advice, warnings, descriptions of remarkable people and a list of vocabulary to converse with foreigners. It became a book that all English seafarers kept on board ship(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The history of love reaches the Victorian age, where Lucy explores the romantic gestures that emerged and still exist today, such as performing love songs and sending flowers. She also reveals how medieval chivalry shaped courtship, defining the roles that men and women had to play, and dips into popular novels of the time, discovering how the passion explored in fiction was translating into real-life desires(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Botticelli's Venus: The Making of an Icon
programme.desc: Sam Roddick explores the enduring appeal of 15th-century painting The Birth of Venus, revealing its journey to worldwide fame was far from straightforward after lying in obscurity for centuries. He examines why Botticelli's nude was so revolutionary, and looks at its impact on modern culture with artists including Terry Gilliam, who memorably re-invented it for his Monty Python's Flying Circus animations(n)

programme.title: Life Drawing Live!
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby hosts a live broadcast of a life drawing class led by artists Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie, offering viewers the chance to draw along with the six amateur artists in the studio as they capture a series of poses. Plus, artist and teacher Diana Ali offers some helpful tips(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna reaches the end of her tether and tries to drive bulimic Delilah out of the house. Warren antagonises Miles by telling him to take an Aids test and Egg feigns injury to evade an increasingly frustrated Milly's amorous advances(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Miles urges Delilah to have an HIV test and Egg, deeply embarrassed at having caused a scene, seeks advice from Warren's therapist. Anna worries she isn't getting enough work at the chambers and hits on the bright idea of using her feminine charms to drum up business(n)

programme.desc: The Dup Dup delivers toys for the friends to play with and even the Noo-noo enjoys a new treat(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore gloves through discovery, play, song and story(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee's nephew Dudley comes to stay. He is a very cute puppy, but looks like he could cause chaos in the clubhouse(n)

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket(n)

programme.title: Mister Maker's Arty Party
programme.desc: Mister Maker and the gang make brightly coloured curly creations with paper and paint, and discover how a whirling spinning wheel made a potter's life much easier(n)

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children(n)

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory meets Millie and her pet chickens, and together they collect fresh eggs and decorate them, before eating some pancakes using the ingredient. Finally, the pair see some chicks hatching in an incubator, and get to hold the baby birds(n)

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wingston's Daddy is running late so while all the other little monsters head home with their parents, a woeful Wingston helps Miss Grizzlesniff fill the goo pit(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: Cameras follow Joseph as he goes on his first camping trip to the Lake District with his family(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: The story of how the vulture lost its feathers when it flew up to the sun(n)

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Would-be chef Ben, whose mum and dad are German, cooks a traditional Zwiebelkuchen following a popular recipe from his parents' homeland(n)

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Miss Bowline-Hitch's sundial needs the sun to shine on it, and Jack recalls a treasure hunt that needed daylight to solve(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The little girl accompanies Mr McColl to visit her Uncle Matthew, who lives over on the other side of the Island and is a bit of a hermit. She is delighted, because she's been feeling rather grumpy towards her little brother Liam and is rather curious to see her uncle(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody discover Granny's broken music box, and Mum gives her Clair De Lune by Debussy, which captures the youngster's imagination(n)

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: The monsters must find the perfect spot for a fussy potted plant who complains about everything - even the curtains(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say lion, prior to a whirlwind word adventure. Plus, how lions look and what they do with a big lion, a baby lion and a roaring lion(n)

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One's idea of fun is singing, dancing and making friends by adding One! Learn to count to 10 by adding one with the Numberblocks(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A talking ride on car seems like a fun toy at Amma's creche, but Bing and Sula must use their imagination when it crashes(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Great BigHoo, Toodloo, Chickedy and Chick get carried away and roll everything from their picnic down a hill(n)
