programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies bottom bounce on The Tubby Bouncy before wiggling their bottoms to a special bottom bump song(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Three young brothers have fun blowing bubbles in the garden(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook claims to be a descendant of royalty and demands to be treated like a king(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog gets a delivery that arrives in a big box and the squirrels discover that cardboard can be very interesting(n)

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The crew tries to rescue Shellington when he gets trapped in the middle of a jellyfish bloom(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is at a shopping centre. She uses special cameras to help find out how an escalator works to move people from one floor to another. An animation shows the hidden cogs and chains inside the escalator which make the steps move. Next, Maddie visits a factory to see how wheelbarrows are made from metal sheets to help people move heavy things around outside. She attaches a special camera to a big robot to see it working(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: A big storm wrecks the rabbits' treehouse, and placing it back together leads to a big adventure(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Wizard Tripwick tries to show his wizarding pupils how to make bananas magically appear, and Captain Adorable vlogs about his latest superhero mission.(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's teacher comes for a home visit and the Brooklyn-Bells want Waffle to be on his best behaviour, but Waffle cannot stop chewing everything(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly and her friends go litter-picking on the beach. Meanwhile, Moira has lost her precious whistle and no-one realises it's in Molly's bucket and about to get recycled(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion shows Pepi Nana his toy bridge. Pepi Nana wishes they could cross the bridge(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny suggests Mother should have a go at creating a tune, but she gets very flustered and rushes off to her garden(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Eee accidentally knocks over Ooo's tower of bricks, causing him to play sad music on the piano(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Hugh Shampoo, by Karen George Hugh hates to have his hair washed, brushed or cut - but his hairdresser parents need his help. Read by Ellie Harrison(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by Cemlyn Davies(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with more moral and religious discussions on topical issues, beginning with an edition from James Allen's Girls' School in Dulwich, south London(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, her discovering how to have a good time. It's a story of horn blowing, bog snorkelling and Elvis impersonators, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores the ruins of Reading Abbey, once one of Europe's finest churches, and the Rev Kate Bottley discovers the deep Christian symbolism of snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale and guests are at the Stade de France to look ahead to this afternoon's match between France and England, and dissect the weekend's other fixtures - which were Ireland v Scotland and Wales v Italy(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley presents a programme following the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, beginning with the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a fire-damaged house in West Sussex, a semi-detached in the West Midlands and a barn conversion in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, making sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and cracking a gold bangle scam in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the origins of an underground seaside grotto(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha hosts her first chronic disease coffee morning, while Daniel tries to get through to Jimmi in prison. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Return of the comedy drama about a pair of Stratford-upon-Avon private detectives, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple returning from 20 years living in America as they look for a home in Hampshire's New Forest near to their children's new school. Sonali also goes behind the scenes during the preparations for a hugely popular traditional country show(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeeper Valentine and market gardener John meet their regular customers and discover new ones selling their skincare products and organic vegetables at St George's weekly market in central Belfast(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram scour Kent for items to sell at auction in Rayleigh, Essex, along the way buying a French sunburst clock and an old school bell(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a rogue trader who is a familiar face on X-Ray, this time selling new roofs for conservatories - and letting down customers again. Lucy Owen is on a mission to find out why her recycling bags are being rejected by her local waste recycling plant and Omar Hamdi finds out how one internet provider appears to have revived the practice of slamming, where customers are switched from one company to another without their knowledge or consent(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda has a run-in with Shelley at the school gates and is furious to discover Mick has cancelled her card - but her day only gets worse from there, leading the drunken landlady to inadvertently put young Ollie in danger. Denise returns home from Scotland and makes amends with Sheree, Jack tells Kat the police have evidence that could help Kush and Iqra invites herself along to a Panesar family meal(n)

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: A Friday evening trip out in the car ends in a fatal crash, but the driver refuses to accept his driving is to blame. The collision team must find the evidence to prove the real story of the night(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thomas investigates the case of John Sealy, a soldier who died during a military training exercise involving exposure to CS gas. It appears the victim died from an aneurysm, but when Thomas discovers the trace of a possible nerve agent in his system, the man's family are desperate for answers. Was a chemical weapon involved, or is there a more innocent explanation? First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme meets some of the growing number of people living in temporary accommodation. With a shortage of council-owned properties, and a desire to keep people out of bed and breakfasts, local authorities are increasingly turning to the private sector for help. In 2013, a change in the law meant companies could buy up and convert old office blocks into homes without planning permission. Reporter Callum Tulley explores life for residents living in these converted spaces(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day(n)

programme.title: Rio Ferdinand: Being Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Former footballer Rio Ferdinand lost his 34-year-old wife Rebecca to cancer in May 2015, and he is still coming to terms with his loss, and the effect it has had upon himself and his three children. Rio meets other families coping with bereavement, and looks at what help is available for parents and children who have experienced loss to move on with their lives(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, who is appearing as Doctor Robotnik in si-fi adventure Sonic the Hedgehog. Also on the sofa is Australian actress Margot Robbie, who features in new DC superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go, and another bunch of intrepid audience members tries to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: An audacious scam robs NHS hospitals of more than £12.5million and a hospital cashier leads the high life, taking money meant for patients and putting it into her own bank account(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a rogue trader who is a familiar face on X-Ray, this time selling new roofs for conservatories - and letting down customers again. programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Jonathan Pratt and Danny Sebastian travel to Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire for the buying and selling contest. Two married couples go head to head to buy three items that will hopefully make a profit at auction, and they all have their sights set on a golden gavel. Eric also visits the small village of Eyam to discover how, 350 years ago, the villagers made a daring decision to quarantine themselves, and stop the spread of the plague(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al tries to cure Bear of his fear of blood, as Daniel and Zara continue to work through their troubled past. On a drug raid, Rob bumps into two familiar faces(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu take on the case of a washed-up rock star who believes that the Devil is trying to kill him, and set out to unmask the real culprit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Worcestershire to find a home in Cornwall, and discovers a project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers Ian and Fiona compete against the Wills family, who run a mixed farm and exhibit pigs, at the Yeovil Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram look for bargains in East Anglia, as well as having a go at step dancing and discovering an 18th-century collection of self-playing instruments(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is racked with guilt about Kush and plans to put things right, despite Tiffany urging her not to do anything that puts her in danger. Shirley is determined to make Linda face her problems and pleads with Mick to take his wife to an addict support session at the community centre. Ash and her brothers are surprised by Suki's reaction to Iqra, Sheree opens up to Patrick about Isaac, and Sharon makes a peace offering to Dennis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Oxford United v Newcastle United (Kick-off 8.05pm). Coverage of the fourth-round replay, which comes from Kassam Stadium, as the teams contest the right to face West Bromwich Albion at The Hawthorns in the next round. The match at St James' Park ended 0-0, with Newcastle forward Joelinton having two goals disallowed for offside, and League One Oxford will now feel they have an excellent chance of producing an upset against their Premier League opponents(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeeper Valentine and market gardener John meet their regular customers and discover new ones selling their skincare products and organic vegetables at St George's weekly market in central Belfast(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram scour Kent for items to sell at auction in Rayleigh, Essex, along the way buying a French sunburst clock and an old school bell(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: John Clark steps aside as skipper to let his 22-year-old son David take the helm of fishing boat Reliance for the first time, while Michael Watt is on the hunt for squid in the Moray Firth in his trawler Sardonyx. Back on shore, the Robertsons are checking their boat Zenith into the dry dock for some vital maintenance work(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda has a run-in with Shelley at the school gates and is furious to discover Mick has cancelled her card - but her day only gets worse from there, leading the drunken landlady to inadvertently put young Ollie in danger. Denise returns home from Scotland and makes amends with Sheree, Jack tells Kat the police have evidence that could help Kush and Iqra invites herself along to a Panesar family meal(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme meets some of the growing number of people living in temporary accommodation. With a shortage of council-owned properties, and a desire to keep people out of bed and breakfasts, local authorities are increasingly turning to the private sector for help. In 2013, a change in the law meant companies could buy up and convert old office blocks into homes without planning permission. Reporter Callum Tulley explores life for residents living in these converted spaces(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thomas investigates the case of John Sealy, a soldier who died during a military training exercise involving exposure to CS gas. It appears the victim died from an aneurysm, but when Thomas discovers the trace of a possible nerve agent in his system, the man's family are desperate for answers. Was a chemical weapon involved, or is there a more innocent explanation? First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day(n)

programme.title: Rio Ferdinand: Being Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Former footballer Rio Ferdinand lost his 34-year-old wife Rebecca to cancer in May 2015, and he is still coming to terms with his loss, and the effect it has had upon himself and his three children. Rio meets other families coping with bereavement, and looks at what help is available for parents and children who have experienced loss to move on with their lives(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, who is appearing as Doctor Robotnik in si-fi adventure Sonic the Hedgehog. Also on the sofa is Australian actress Margot Robbie, who features in new DC superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go, and another bunch of intrepid audience members tries to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: An audacious scam robs NHS hospitals of more than £12.5million and a hospital cashier leads the high life, taking money meant for patients and putting it into her own bank account(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A learner driver tries to cheat his way through his driving test using a Bluetooth device, while a wealthy businessman sells unlicensed medicines that he claims can cure cancer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Jonathan Pratt and Danny Sebastian travel to Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire for the buying and selling contest. Two married couples go head to head to buy three items that will hopefully make a profit at auction, and they all have their sights set on a golden gavel. Eric also visits the small village of Eyam to discover how, 350 years ago, the villagers made a daring decision to quarantine themselves, and stop the spread of the plague(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al tries to cure Bear of his fear of blood, as Daniel and Zara continue to work through their troubled past. On a drug raid, Rob bumps into two familiar faces(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu take on the case of a washed-up rock star who believes that the Devil is trying to kill him, and set out to unmask the real culprit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Worcestershire to find a home in Cornwall, and discovers a project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers Ian and Fiona compete against the Wills family, who run a mixed farm and exhibit pigs, at the Yeovil Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram look for bargains in East Anglia, as well as having a go at step dancing and discovering an 18th-century collection of self-playing instruments(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is racked with guilt about Kush and plans to put things right, despite Tiffany urging her not to do anything that puts her in danger. Shirley is determined to make Linda face her problems and pleads with Mick to take his wife to an addict support session at the community centre. Ash and her brothers are surprised by Suki's reaction to Iqra, Sheree opens up to Patrick about Isaac, and Sharon makes a peace offering to Dennis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Oxford United v Newcastle United (Kick-off 8.05pm). Coverage of the fourth-round replay, which comes from Kassam Stadium, as the teams contest the right to face West Bromwich Albion at The Hawthorns in the next round. The match at St James' Park ended 0-0, with Newcastle forward Joelinton having two goals disallowed for offside, and League One Oxford will now feel they have an excellent chance of producing an upset against their Premier League opponents(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with more moral and religious discussions on topical issues, beginning with an edition from James Allen's Girls' School in Dulwich, south London(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a third-floor flat in Walworth, central London, and a block of flats in Hengoed in South Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant help teams searching for bargains in Kingston upon Thames, and host Charlie Ross finds out about a Victorian photographer who hailed from the town(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores the ruins of Reading Abbey, once one of Europe's finest churches, and the Rev Kate Bottley discovers the deep Christian symbolism of snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale and guests are at the Stade de France to look ahead to this afternoon's match between France and England, and dissect the weekend's other fixtures - which were Ireland v Scotland and Wales v Italy(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Jennifer Reoch and Joe Lindsay head to the Kvarner Gulf in northern Croatia. They exolore the holiday resort of Opatija, a hotel whose famous guests included playwright Anton Chekhov and dancer Isadora Duncan, and take a day trip to the island of Kirk, where they try a sparkling wine which is fermented in an unusual way. For the short break, Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark introduces her holiday favourite, the Isle of Arran on Scotland's west coast, where Brodick Castle is the first call on her itinerary(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the Belfast Community Gospel Choir's preparation for its annual winter concert, exploring the stories of members of the group. The programme talks to members of the group and reveals their reasons for joining(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following a group of Hindu women in Belfast, who choose to honour their husbands by praying and fasting on one day each autumn(n)

programme.desc: Musicians from various genres team up for one-off performances, including Kitt Philippa with Daithí and the Belfast Community Gospel Choir, Wood Burning Savages with Ursula Burns, Ruth McGinley with Neil Martin, and The Lost Brothers with Zoe Conway. programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enjoys one of Britain's National Trails as she walks part of the Thames Path through Oxfordshire - meeting some of the people and animals who call the banks of this impressive river home. Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a special double elimination show, in which two more singers will be unmasked. Sharon and Kelly Osbourne are today's special guests on the panel, joining series regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall as they attempt to guess the identities of the six famous faces performing from behind bizarre costumes(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform fairy-tale routines, hoping to get a high enough score from the judges and viewers to keep them out of the bottom two. Sadly there'll be no happy ever after for Radzi Chinyanganya, who lost the skate-off last week, but while the votes are being counted there will be a routine from Disney on Ice. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. This time, the actor and his son arrive in Ohio, where they head for Dayton - the birthplace of aviation - before the pair attend a local rodeo later that evening. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: England v France. Jill Douglas presents action from both teams' opening match of the championship, which came from Stade de France. programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Museum of the Family is packed with visitors to see a collection of miniature dolls houses donated by a real estate mogul - only for the businessman to be shot dead in front of the crowd. programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: The funerals approach, and without the answers he needs and still more questions being thrown up from White House Farm, Stan's efforts remain fruitless. programme.desc: When a popular vlogger gets trapped during an avalanche, International Rescue must find a way to carefully dig him out(n)

programme.desc: Po discovers that he has been poisoned and sets off to find who is behind it, while the students follow him in secret.(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a tour of Tobago, where he puts his own spin on some local recipes, including hand clapped rotis, dougla rum chicken, mustard fish and mango chili chow, and a bluewater breeze cocktail. He also helps Charlottesville fishermen to bring in the day's catch and takes a relaxing boat trip to the Nylon Pool(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up crispy party poppers with barbecue dipping sauce - ideal for movie nights - a breakfast burrito, aubergine curry and banana bread doughnuts. They are also joined by Prue Leith, who tucks into their mushroom wellington(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Bert, a bouncy Great Dane puppy who is suffering growing pains, and needs surgery to prevent his front legs developing in an abnormal fashion. He also hears how caucasian shepherd dog Georgie is struggling to find a home due to her unusual appearance - her ears have been docked, giving her a scary look. Paul thinks a few website pictures might do the trick and calls on leading fashion photographer Rankin to help out. Finally, an injured Jack Russell is found by a council warden and rushed straight into surgery. Even if the operation is a success, can the dog hope to get over such a nasty experience?(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Labrador-lurcher Pluto, who has a rare condition that means he cannot swallow food properly. As a result he is not putting on any weight, leaving the Battersea vets scratching their heads wondering how to ensure he has a long and healthy life. Meanwhile, German spitz Hayes is scaring off prospective owners with his scary fang-filled smile, and when two-year-old cavachon Miley is dropped off at the home, she becomes terrified of her new surroundings. Can Paul cheer her up?(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: An African Journey
programme.desc: When the Duke and Duchess of Sussex took baby son Archie on their recent tour of southern Africa, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby was with them throughout. Here Bradby gains intimate access to the royal couple, speaking to them about the causes and issues they care most about and exploring some of the pressures and challenges they face as a young family in the glare of the world's media(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Lydia and Ruby visit a convent to make a seemingly miraculous diagnosis, but the treatment of a young nun and the senior doctor's interfering attitude creates tension between them, which finally erupts in a fierce confrontation. Maggie and Paul join a group of pilgrims on a trek through the jungle, hoping to find the answer to their prayers. Guest starring Madhur Jaffrey(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The monsoon season is beginning, and as the first rains start to fall, the tension between Lydia and Ruby is reaching boiling point - driving the younger doctor to make a shocking decision. Maggie's health begins to decline dramatically, and Debbie is adamant that the family must return to the UK. However, Maggie remains determined to remain in India for the time she has left, and struggles to persuade her daughter to honour her last wishes. programme.title: Law & Order: UK

With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bernie becomes the target of a series of mysterious and violent attacks, while Peggy is forced to intervene after David finds he can't match the other contestants in an angling competition. Helen is horrified by the arrival of her estranged father in Aidensfield - but he has a bombshell to drop that causes her to rethink their relationship. Rural drama, starring Peter Benson and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Cavalier King Charles spaniels Lady and Gemma, elderly sisters who have spent their lives in each other's company. It is hoped they will find a new home together - but then things take a turn for the worse when the vets realise they also need surgery on their ears. Will they get the retirement they deserve? Also arriving is emaciated mongrel Hector, who is desperately underweight, while Paul tries to cheer up Alaskan malamute Baloo, the home's longest-serving resident, by giving the depressed dog a change of scene(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition of the series, dedicated to the worldwide search for the remains of soldiers killed during the First World War with no known graves. The programme follows the work of an elite all-female team at the Ministry of Defence known as the 'War Detectives', whose job is to locate the final resting places of 500,000 service personnel who lost their lives during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama starring Bradley Walsh. Ronnie and new partner Joe lead the investigation into the death of jeweller Harry Bernstein, who has been found with his hands and teeth missing. His wife, her lover and a former business associate are all possible suspects, but the case takes a surprising turn when the victim's sister receives a gruesome delivery. She's on the jury for the trial of a drug dealer and murderer who Ronnie has been trying to nail for years, and the threat to her points to jury tampering, so Jake and Kate have to take a dramatic approach to win the case. The problem is, they're up against a formidable defence barrister. Co-starring Ben Bailey Smith, Dominic Rowan and Georgia Taylor, with guests Helen Baxendale and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a dismal holiday in Spain to find a surprise awaiting him - the love of his life, Miss Jones, is back - with a baby in tow. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tim tries to persuade Charlie to leave the Rovers, Ali is on hand as Maria suffers a heartbreaking loss. Daniel confuses Bethany for Sinead and kisses her(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham's body is discovered, Charity is floored by what she learns, and Rhona is intrigued by something she finds(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Bert, a bouncy Great Dane puppy who is suffering growing pains, and needs surgery to prevent his front legs developing in an abnormal fashion. He also hears how caucasian shepherd dog Georgie is struggling to find a home due to her unusual appearance - her ears have been docked, giving her a scary look. Paul thinks a few website pictures might do the trick and calls on leading fashion photographer Rankin to help out. Finally, an injured Jack Russell is found by a council warden and rushed straight into surgery. Even if the operation is a success, can the dog hope to get over such a nasty experience?(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Labrador-lurcher Pluto, who has a rare condition that means he cannot swallow food properly. As a result he is not putting on any weight, leaving the Battersea vets scratching their heads wondering how to ensure he has a long and healthy life. Meanwhile, German spitz Hayes is scaring off prospective owners with his scary fang-filled smile, and when two-year-old cavachon Miley is dropped off at the home, she becomes terrified of her new surroundings. Can Paul cheer her up?(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: An African Journey
programme.desc: When the Duke and Duchess of Sussex took baby son Archie on their recent tour of southern Africa, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby was with them throughout. Here Bradby gains intimate access to the royal couple, speaking to them about the causes and issues they care most about and exploring some of the pressures and challenges they face as a young family in the glare of the world's media(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Lydia and Ruby visit a convent to make a seemingly miraculous diagnosis, but the treatment of a young nun and the senior doctor's interfering attitude creates tension between them, which finally erupts in a fierce confrontation. Maggie and Paul join a group of pilgrims on a trek through the jungle, hoping to find the answer to their prayers. Guest starring Madhur Jaffrey(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The monsoon season is beginning, and as the first rains start to fall, the tension between Lydia and Ruby is reaching boiling point - driving the younger doctor to make a shocking decision. Maggie's health begins to decline dramatically, and Debbie is adamant that the family must return to the UK. However, Maggie remains determined to remain in India for the time she has left, and struggles to persuade her daughter to honour her last wishes. Conclusion of the medical drama, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam lead the investigation into the death of a wealthy woman whose body was dumped in the Thames. The team initially focuses inquiries on the victim's abusive ex-husband, with whom she had been involved in a bitter struggle over custody of their daughter Holly, but the case heads in a different direction when the detectives discover the teenager was infatuated with her mother's new partner. With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Drama set in 1940 at a five-star London hotel, showing the impact of the Second World War on families, politics, relationships and work across every social strata. The Halcyon's owner Lord Hamilton holds a secret meeting that attracts the interest of American guest Joe O'Hara, while manager Richard Garland must deal with the arrival of Lady Hamilton a day earlier than expected. Starring Olivia Williams, Steven Mackintosh, Alex Jennings and Matt Ryan(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham's body is discovered, Charity is floored by what she learns, and Rhona is intrigued by something she finds(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones falls for new guest Hilary, a budding playwright who sends Rigsby into fits of jealousy by offering her the starring role in his latest masterpiece. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and guest Peter Bowles(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy is put out when Curly coldly accepts his resignation. Mike is horrified when Steve rents the flat next to him. Samir gets a job, working at a Moroccan restaurant(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet is furious with Charlie for telling Tanya about Raquel's misfortune. Curly cooks Raquel a meal and tries to buck her up. Samir starts work at the Casablanca restaurant(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is counting the cost after her pop concert. Bernard's suggestion leaves his family aghast. Frank returns from hospital with something to say to Kim(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank struggles with the quiet life, and Seth fights back against enforced retirement. On the day of the Post Office trial, Alan's world falls apart when he hears the evidence(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's niece comes to stay in Aidensfield following the breakdown of her marriage, but immediately suffers another blow when her prize racehorse is kidnapped and held to ransom. Romance is in the air for Rosie as a biker gang turns up in town, and a seedy photographer promises to make a star of Phil Bellamy's stepdaughter Jane. Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby lets a room to a young couple he believes have just got married - unaware that they are not man and wife. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local farmer Colin Judd is devastated when he is forced to place his property in the hands of an auctioneer following the death of his father - but is even more shocked to see his estranged brother at the sale. Debbie Bellamy finds herself in trouble for drink-driving, and David has difficulty with a stubborn pony meant to be pulling a trap full of tourists. Rural drama, with David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim asserts herself over the Home Farm staff at the bike rally. Turner is appalled at the state of the Woolpack's books. Luke confronts the Dingle family once again(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris fails to rekindle the romance in his marriage. Betty discovers the full extent of Pollard's deception. At Emmerdale, the Beast of Beckindale strikes again(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy and Curly agree to be friends again, Bet is pleased when Raquel returns to work behind the bar. Tracy tells Deirdre she is disgusted by her and her toy boy(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre tells Tracy about Denise being pregnant. Charlie makes plans to leave Weatherfield, but Tanya persuades him to face Bet and tell the truth about their affair(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Louisa opens a new boarding house, but the arrival of a mysterious new guest places the villa under police scrutiny. Gerry remains as devoted as ever to the island's wildlife, and announces his plans to open his own zoo. Margo is struggling to find clients for her beauty treatments, and resorts to desperate measures to find people to practice her skills on. The final series of the drama based on Gerald Durrell's books, starring Keeley Hawes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson stars as the Parisian detective in this drama based on the novels of Georges Simenon. The summer of 1955 finds Paris in the grip of both sweltering heat and terror on the streets. Four women have been murdered in Montmartre, with no apparent connection between the victims. Chief Inspector Maigret comes under huge pressure from the public and his superiors to apprehend the killer before he can strike again. Fiona Shaw and Mark Heap head up the supporting cast(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Following the brutal murder of a gay man, Sam and Ronnie embark on a desperate mission to track down the victim's adoptive son, who has gone missing. The prime suspect soon emerges as the child's biological father - an alleged violent homophobe unable to stomach the idea of the youngster being brought up in a same-sex household - but was he acting alone? Guest starring Glynis Barber, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.desc: An encounter with Daphne leaves Leslie pondering the possibility of fatherhood. programme.title: Hornblower

programme.title: Darts: The Masters Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of the semi-finals and final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Michael van Gerwen continued his domination of this event in 2019, defeating Dave Chisnall in the last four and 2014 champion James Wade in the showpiece match to win the title for the fifth year in succession. However, Peter Wright's dominant victory over van Gerwen in January's World Championship final demonstrated the Dutchman is not invincible, and others will fancy their chances of taking this title. With analysis from Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little, and commentary by John Rawling, Stuart Pyke and Ned Boulting(n)

programme.title: Steven Gerrard: Make Us Dream (2018)

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water that's sliced cleanly through a young man's testicles, is enough to lure 'fishing detective' Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating(n)

programme.title: Australia on Fire: Climate Emergency
programme.desc: The dramatic and terrifying story of the battle to save Australia from the bushfires ravaging the country, including first-hand accounts from firefighters on the front line. Reporter Kylie Morris examines the long-term impact of the fires on the diverse animal population and ecology, with drone footage revealing the extent of the devastation, while the programme also features testimony from those who only just escaped their burning homes with their lives, as well as hearing from some of those who have returned to burnt-out properties(n)

In severe heat, the tracks can expand and with fears of them buckling, it falls to the team in Network Rail's Swindon control centre to manage the situation, and they are left with little choice but to issue speed restrictions of just 5mph(n)

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Home and Away
